text.title: Edge and CDN Strategy Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Edge and CDN Strategy application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Choose edge platform on three axes: (a) compute runtime (V8 isolates / containers / Wasm), (b) cold-start behavior, (c) origin proximity. Pick ONE, do not multi-cloud edge.
rule.rationale: Edge platforms compose poorly; multi-edge multiplies the failure modes without proportional benefit.

rule.statement: Cache key = path + query (allowlist) + Accept-Encoding + Vary headers. Wildcards in keys cause silent cache poisoning.
rule.rationale: Cache key design is the #1 CDN bug source; explicit allowlist prevents poisoning + leakage.

rule.statement: Origin shield (intermediate cache layer) is mandatory for any service with ≥1k req/min to origin. Shields reduce origin load 50-90%.
rule.rationale: Without shield, traffic spikes hit origin; shield is the cheapest insurance against origin overload.

rule.statement: Edge auth (JWT verification, signed cookies) supplements origin auth; never replaces. Origin must independently validate.
rule.rationale: Edge can be bypassed (direct origin requests); double validation is the only safe pattern.

rule.statement: Purge strategy: targeted purge per object > tag-based purge > wildcard purge > full purge. Wildcard purges should require approval.
rule.rationale: Full purges cause global cache stampedes; explicit hierarchy prevents accidental DDoS-on-self.
